subject: Gaming
title: Predatory Monetization of Children
description: A fast breakdown of Predatory Monetization of Children for builders.

hook.text: Children can't distinguish ads from gameplay. Games exploit this to sell virtual items to kids.

buildup.text: Free-to-play kids' games embed purchases into gameplay. Pop-ups interrupt with 'buy now' at critical moments.

discovery.text: Children under 12 lack the cognitive development to understand manipulative monetization techniques.

twist.text: Stories of children spending thousands on parents' credit cards are common. Refunds are rarely automatic.

climax.text: Regulators are slowly acting. Age-gated spending and parental controls are becoming mandatory.

punchline.text: Kids can't tell ads from gameplay. The industry profits from confusion.

quiz.question: Why are children particularly vulnerable to gaming monetization?
options: They lack the cognitive ability to distinguish ads from gameplay
They have more money than adults
They play more hours than adults
